lessons.text: Notice fear grip your body before a hard task.
Some wins bring only guilt.
No prize is worth ruining people you love.
Others' greed is no excuse for your own.
One reckless act can wound generations.
Don't let despair talk you into giving up.
Drop the self-pity and stand up.
When you're lost, ask someone wiser.
Don't let grief run your life.
You remain yourself through every stage of life.
Pleasure and pain both pass; ride them out.

lessons.note: Pure metaphysics (transient vs. permanent) with no actionable kernel.
Metaphysics of the indestructible self; not universal daily wisdom.
Metaphysics of the unkillable self; abstract, not actionable.
Reincarnation imagery; scriptural cosmology, not modern wisdom.
Deathless indweller in all beings; metaphysical, not daily wisdom.

lessons.text: Grief can't undo what was always coming.
Don't shrink from what's yours to do.
Running from your work costs your good name.
Treat win and loss the same, then act.
No honest effort is ever wasted.
One clear aim beats a dozen scattered ones.
Chasing every reward keeps your mind restless.
Don't be ruled by highs, lows, or wanting more.
Direct understanding outgrows secondhand answers.
Your right is to your work, not its rewards.
Stay steady through both success and failure.
Skill is working wisely, not just working hard.
Let go of the payoff and you're free.
See clearly and empty talk loses its pull.
Clarity comes when your mind stops wavering.
Real contentment comes from within, not from getting.
Stay steady through sorrow, craving, and fear.
Don't cling to good news or flinch at bad.
Pull your attention back when the world overwhelms.
Willpower stops the act; insight ends the craving.
Even the disciplined get pulled off by impulse.
Master your impulses and keep your aim high.
Dwelling on wanting breeds desire, then anger, then ruin.
Meet temptation calmly, neither grabbing nor fleeing.
No peace or joy without a steady mind.
One stray impulse drags your focus off course.
What everyone chases, the wise often question.
Let desires come and go like rivers to the sea.
Let go of craving, ownership, and ego.
True peace holds steady even at the end.
You can't not act, so act well.
Don't fake discipline while your mind wanders.
Doing something beats doing nothing.
Work as a contribution, not just for gain.
Give back to whatever sustains you.
Keep giving back, or your life runs empty.
When you're whole within, you need nothing to prove.
Do what must be done without clinging.
People copy what their leaders actually do.
Don't quit just because you've nothing left to gain.
Do good calmly, without unsettling others.
Ease up — you're not the only force at work.
Act freely, without ego or feverish worry.
Follow good guidance with trust, not cynicism.
Don't let likes and dislikes run your choices.
Better your own path flawed than another's perfect.
Desire and anger are your real enemies.
Craving clouds judgment like smoke hides fire.
First control the impulse, then uproot the craving.
